truth.rule: Current Spec Kit artifacts are the truth source for session worker execution.

work.session_model_rule: Codex sessions are workers, not memory.

worker_lanes.description: Own the current constitution or policy baseline before downstream specification or planning work starts.
Own feature specification truth and requirement updates anchored to the current Spec Kit slice.
Own plan/task derivation and dependency sequencing from the current specification truth.
Own governance docs, dev-core routing docs, runbooks, reference docs, and redacted evidence docs without broadening runtime or deploy scope.
Own auth_core and related identity/session implementation or review slices gated by the registered auth role.
Own bears_gateway implementation or review slices gated by the registered gateway role.
Own cd_deploy_stage and Kubernetes deploy-core work with rollback, truth-contract, evidence, and operator-approval requirements kept explicit.
Own validator, test, redacted observability, emulator, and E2E evidence work that checks the current slice without redefining scope.
Own review-only or risk-evidence work with explicit closeout and no hidden implementation scope.
Own independent audit work with a fresh session, no parent context, and no implementation authority.
Own one bounded implementation or migration-proof slice that is already approved and role-gated for the selected exact target surface.

runtime_artifacts.name: session-workers.json
runtime_artifacts.description: Worker registry with per-worker scope, Spec Kit snapshot, and packet references.

runtime_artifacts.name: orchestration-state.json
runtime_artifacts.description: Runtime state summary keyed by worker state with explicit scope-lock reference.

runtime_artifacts.name: worker-heartbeat.json
runtime_artifacts.description: Per-worker heartbeat packet for status, target evidence, and current step summary.

runtime_artifacts.name: worker-closeout.json
runtime_artifacts.description: Per-worker closeout packet with changed files, validation commands, and resume guidance.

runtime_artifacts.name: scope-locks.json
runtime_artifacts.description: Lock registry that prevents overlapping write scopes across live workers.

runtime_artifacts.name: session-reuse-index.json
runtime_artifacts.description: Deterministic reuse registry keyed by lane, role, scope, repo state, Spec Kit snapshot, and roadmap slice.

resume_fork_rule.rule: Historical session resume, reuse, or fork is allowed only when goal_id, roadmap_id, lane, role, scope, current repo state, current Spec Kit snapshot, and roadmap slice match; session reuse or fork also requires validate-runtime success before the action; otherwise spawn fresh with current Spec Kit truth plus bounded prior evidence.
resume_fork_rule.fresh_fallback: spawn fresh session with bounded prior evidence when compatibility or validate-runtime fails

implementation_lane_policy.rule: /speckit-implement is one controlled implementation lane, not a global executor.

concurrency_policy.rule: Concurrent workers may develop multiple Spec Kit specifications only when each worker binds exactly one spec_id, one spec_path, and one current Spec Kit snapshot.
concurrency_policy.scope_rule: Concurrent workers for different spec_id values are valid only when scope locks do not overlap.
concurrency_policy.fanout_preflight_rule: Before parent fanout, count active workers and open workers from session-workers.json plus orchestration-state.json, close completed no-longer-needed workers, reserve critical-path wait slots, spawn bounded batches when requested workers exceed available slots, and classify thread-limit spawn failure as WORKFLOW_DRIFT instead of normal recovery.

capacity_fallback_reconciliation.rule: Before capacity fallback or replacement launch, parent must reconcile partial subagent state from session tail, dirty checkout scan, and open PR checks into deterministic buckets; unknown or completed-open agents are not free capacity.

buckets.required_action: Keep waiting, interrupt, or explicitly close before replacement launch.
Inspect closeout, PR URLs, validation evidence, and dirty files; integrate or close before replacement launch.
Review session tail, tool outputs, checkout state, and open PRs before deciding replacement or quarantine.
Refresh worker status, session tail, checkout state, and GitHub branch or PR state before replacement launch.
Parent must resolve, rescope, quarantine, or explicitly close the blocked worker before replacement launch.

capacity_fallback_reconciliation.free_capacity_rule: Only closed workers are free capacity; unknown, completed-open, failed, blocked, active, dirty-checkout, or open-PR workers are not free capacity.
capacity_fallback_reconciliation.duplicate_launch_rule: Block duplicate worker launch for the same task id until the partial-state check is clean and the prior worker is integrated, quarantined, or closed.
required_evidence_checks: Inspect worker session tail for task_complete, PR URLs, and last tool outputs.
Check the declared checkout or worktree for dirty files.
Check GitHub for open PRs from the worker branch.

session_reuse_index.rule: The reuse_key must be deterministic from key_fields and must match goal_id, roadmap_id, and the session-reuse-index.json entry before resume, reuse, or fork; every spawn attempt must record a selection_decision of reuse, fresh, or close_then_fresh; restricted_data_taint blocks reuse; reuse or fork requires validate-runtime success before the action.

audit_lane_policy.rule: Audit lane workers must start fresh with context_policy=fresh_no_parent_context, no parent context, parent_context_allowed=false, no parent_worker_id, and reuse disallowed.

wait_checkpoint_policy.target_mismatch_rule: After every wait_agent, parent must compare returned status keys to requested target ids. If no requested target id appears, parent must emit WAIT_AGENT_TARGET_MISMATCH with requested ids, returned ids, and next_safe_action; stage_advance_allowed must be false; parent must keep waiting for, interrupt, or explicitly close the originally requested agents before dependent work.
wait_checkpoint_policy.stage_advance_rule: Parent may advance dependent work only when at least one requested target id appears in the returned status keys; unrelated returned ids are out-of-band notifications, not valid wait results.
wait_checkpoint_policy.blocker_rule: blocker_escalation is allowed only for missing access, permissions, credentials, ROLE_COVERAGE_BLOCKER, or explicit linked-contract or user stop/rescope that prevents safe progress.
wait_checkpoint_policy.final_integration_rule: Final integration must cite evidence artifacts; plain waiting is not accepted as integration evidence.
